hero.fileTitle: File:A giant Galápagos tortoise (Chelonoidis niger donfaustoi).jpg
hero.alt: A massive adult Galapagos giant tortoise standing in grassy green vegetation
hero.caption: The Galapagos giant tortoise is the largest living species of tortoise on Earth.

habitat.alt: A giant tortoise cooling off in a muddy water wallow
habitat.caption: They inhabit both dry lowlands and humid highlands, often wallowing in mud to cool off.

diet.alt: A Galapagos giant tortoise feeding on green plants and grass
diet.caption: They are herbivores that eat grass, leaves, cacti, lichens, and berries.

baby.alt: A small juvenile Galapagos giant tortoise walking on volcanic soil
baby.caption: Hatchling tortoises emerge from underground nests and are about the size of a tennis ball.

family.alt: A group of giant tortoises gathered in a grassy clearing near trees
family.caption: While solitary, tortoises often gather in groups in grassy feeding pastures or mud pools.

range.fileTitle: File:Galápagos giant tortoise, moving (Chelonoidis niger hoodensis).jpg
range.alt: A giant tortoise walking slowly with legs extended on volcanic soil
range.caption: They walk very slowly, traveling at a speed of about 0.16 miles per hour (0.26 km/h).

size.fileTitle: File:Galápagos tortoise at Asilo de la Paz on Floreana Island.jpg
size.alt: A massive Galapagos tortoise standing in a field next to a fence, showcasing its enormous size
size.caption: These giants can weigh up to 900 pounds (417 kg) and grow to over 5 feet long.

closeup.fileTitle: File:Galapagos giant tortoise, head (Chelonoidis niger hoodensis).jpg
closeup.alt: Close-up of a giant tortoise's wrinkled head and neck, showing its scale-like skin
closeup.caption: Tortoises have sharp, toothless beaks that they use to bite and tear tough vegetation.

fun-fact.alt: A Galapagos giant tortoise stretching its long neck to reach leaves
fun-fact.caption: Galapagos tortoises are among the longest-lived vertebrates, often living over 100 to 150 years!

core-habitat.alt: A giant tortoise in a humid upland meadow
core-habitat.caption: Highlands with moist grass are their preferred feeding spots during the dry season.

core-diet.alt: A giant tortoise feeding on fallen fruits and foliage
core-diet.caption: Their slow metabolism allows them to go up to a year without food or water.

core-family.alt: Two giant tortoises resting near each other
core-family.caption: Tortoises co-exist peacefully, often sleeping near one another.

core-baby.alt: A young Galapagos tortoise feeding on green plants
core-baby.caption: Young tortoises have soft shells and must hide from birds like hawks for their first few years.

core-range.alt: A giant tortoise moving along a dirt path
core-range.caption: They follow well-defined paths through the islands' volcanic terrain.
